stemHtml: GPS indications (without augmentation) may sometimes provide an inaccurate representation of altitude. Why is that?

options.a: If there is an insufficient number of satellites in view, this can result in missing information and inconsistencies with the ephemeris data.
options.b: The height of the ionosphere interferes with the path lengths of the radio waves.
options.c: If there are too many satellites in view, the excess information fails to coincide with that in the Almanac.
options.d: The mathematical model used by the GPS is a very close approximation to, rather than a complete match with, the actual shape of the Earth.
